---
title: "MIAC Baseball 2026 — Modeling"
author: "Ben Trutwin"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = FALSE)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(glmnet)      # LASSO
library(BeSS)        # Best Subset Selection
library(randomForest)
library(pROC)        # ROC / AUC
library(caret)       # confusionMatrix
```

---

## 1. Load & Prepare Modeling Dataset

```{r Load-Data}
df <- read.csv("miac_modeling_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
cat("Raw rows:", nrow(df), "| Raw columns:", ncol(df), "\n")
```

```{r Select-Features}
# Select only pre-game features to avoid data leakage.
# Excluded: home_score, away_score, home_runs, away_runs, run_diff,
#           result, winner, status — all are computed from the game outcome.
feature_cols <- c(
  # Game context
  "is_conference",
  "home_field",
  # Rolling run / win features (last 5 games)
  "home_rf_last5", "home_ra_last5", "home_win_last5", "home_rd_last5",
  "away_rf_last5", "away_ra_last5", "away_win_last5", "away_rd_last5",
  # Rest days
  "home_rest_days", "away_rest_days",
  # Rolling starter IP
  "home_sp_ip_last5", "away_sp_ip_last5",
  # Rolling ERA proxy (runs allowed per 9 starter innings)
  "home_era_proxy_last5", "away_era_proxy_last5",
  # Season pitching quality
  "home_era_season", "home_whip_season", "home_k_per9_season",
  "home_bb_per9_season", "home_opp_ba_season",
  "away_era_season", "away_whip_season", "away_k_per9_season",
  "away_bb_per9_season", "away_opp_ba_season",
  # Season fielding quality
  "home_fld_pct_season", "home_errors_season", "home_dp_season",
  "home_sba_season", "home_csb_season",
  "away_fld_pct_season", "away_errors_season", "away_dp_season",
  "away_sba_season", "away_csb_season"
)

target_col <- "home_win"

# Keep only features + target that actually exist in the data
feature_cols <- intersect(feature_cols, names(df))
model_df <- df %>% select(all_of(c(feature_cols, target_col)))

cat("Feature columns selected:", length(feature_cols), "\n")
```

```{r Drop-NA-Columns}
# Drop features where more than 50% of values are NA
na_rate <- colMeans(is.na(model_df))
dropped <- names(na_rate[na_rate > 0.50 & names(na_rate) != target_col])
cat("Dropping high-NA columns (>50% missing):\n")
print(dropped)

model_df <- model_df %>% select(-all_of(dropped))
cat("Remaining columns:", ncol(model_df) - 1, "features +", target_col, "\n")
```

```{r Drop-Incomplete-Rows}
# Drop rows with any remaining NAs (complete-case analysis)
model_df <- model_df %>% drop_na()
cat("Rows after complete-case filter:", nrow(model_df), "\n")

# Ensure target is a factor for classification models
model_df <- model_df %>%
  mutate(home_win = factor(home_win, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Loss", "Win")))

table(model_df$home_win)
```

---

## 2. Train / Test Split

```{r Train-Test-Split}
set.seed(42)
n <- nrow(model_df)
train_idx <- sample(seq_len(n), size = floor(0.8 * n))

train_df <- model_df[train_idx, ]
test_df  <- model_df[-train_idx, ]

cat("Training rows:", nrow(train_df), "| Test rows:", nrow(test_df), "\n")

# Matrix versions for glmnet / BeSS
X_train <- model.matrix(home_win ~ . - 1, data = train_df)
y_train <- as.numeric(train_df$home_win) - 1   # 0/1

X_test  <- model.matrix(home_win ~ . - 1, data = test_df)
y_test  <- as.numeric(test_df$home_win) - 1
```

---

## 3. Helper — Evaluation Function

```{r Eval-Function}
# Returns accuracy, AUC, and prints a confusion matrix for a given
# vector of predicted probabilities and true binary labels.
evaluate_model <- function(probs, truth, model_name, threshold = 0.5) {
  preds <- ifelse(probs >= threshold, 1, 0)
  acc   <- mean(preds == truth)
  roc_obj <- roc(truth, probs, quiet = TRUE)
  auc_val  <- as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))

  cat("\n===", model_name, "===\n")
  cat("Accuracy:", round(acc, 4), "\n")
  cat("AUC:     ", round(auc_val, 4), "\n")

  cm <- confusionMatrix(
    factor(preds, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Loss", "Win")),
    factor(truth,  levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Loss", "Win")),
    positive = "Win"
  )
  print(cm$table)
  cat("Sensitivity (Recall):", round(cm$byClass["Sensitivity"], 4), "\n")
  cat("Specificity:         ", round(cm$byClass["Specificity"], 4), "\n")

  invisible(list(acc = acc, auc = auc_val, roc = roc_obj, cm = cm))
}
```

---

## 4. Logistic Regression

```{r Logistic-Regression}
log_model <- glm(home_win ~ ., data = train_df, family = binomial)
summary(log_model)

log_probs <- predict(log_model, newdata = test_df, type = "response")
log_eval  <- evaluate_model(log_probs, y_test, "Logistic Regression")
```

---

## 5. LASSO (Regularized Logistic Regression)

```{r LASSO}
# Cross-validated LASSO — alpha = 1 for pure LASSO penalty
set.seed(42)
lasso_cv <- cv.glmnet(X_train, y_train, family = "binomial", alpha = 1, nfolds = 10)

# Best lambda (1 SE rule for parsimony)
best_lambda <- lasso_cv$lambda.1se
cat("Best lambda (1 SE):", round(best_lambda, 6), "\n")

# Coefficients at best lambda (non-zero = selected)
lasso_coef <- coef(lasso_cv, s = "lambda.1se")
selected   <- rownames(lasso_coef)[lasso_coef[, 1] != 0]
cat("\nLASSO-selected features (", length(selected) - 1, "):\n")
print(selected[selected != "(Intercept)"])

lasso_probs <- as.numeric(predict(lasso_cv, newx = X_test,
                                  s = "lambda.1se", type = "response"))
lasso_eval  <- evaluate_model(lasso_probs, y_test, "LASSO")

# Plot CV curve
plot(lasso_cv, main = "LASSO Cross-Validation")
```

```{r LASSO-Coef-Plot}
# Visualise LASSO coefficients
lasso_coef_df <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(lasso_coef)) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  setNames(c("feature", "coefficient")) %>%
  filter(feature != "(Intercept)", coefficient != 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

ggplot(lasso_coef_df, aes(x = reorder(feature, coefficient), y = coefficient)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "LASSO Selected Coefficients",
       x = "Feature", y = "Coefficient") +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## 6. Best Subset Selection (BeSS)

```{r BeSS}
# BeSS fits logistic regression with exact best subset selection.
# We search over subset sizes 1 through 10 to keep it interpretable.
set.seed(42)
bess_model <- bess(X_train, y_train, family = "binomial",
                   method = "sequential", s.min = 1, s.max = 10)

# Find the best subset size by BIC
best_idx <- which.min(bess_model$ic)
best_size <- bess_model$s.list[best_idx]
cat("BeSS optimal subset size (BIC):", best_size, "\n")

# Extract selected variable names from the beta matrix
bess_coef_vec  <- bess_model$beta[, best_idx]
selected_vars  <- names(bess_coef_vec)[bess_coef_vec != 0]
cat("Selected variables:\n")
print(selected_vars)

# Predict from the full bess object — returns a matrix (one column per subset size)
bess_probs_mat <- predict(bess_model, newdata = X_test)
if (is.matrix(bess_probs_mat)) {
  bess_probs <- as.numeric(bess_probs_mat[, best_idx])
} else {
  bess_probs <- as.numeric(bess_probs_mat)
}
cat("bess_probs length:", length(bess_probs), "| y_test length:", length(y_test), "\n")
cat("Any NA in bess_probs?", any(is.na(bess_probs)), "\n")
cat("Any NA in y_test?", any(is.na(y_test)), "\n")
cat("Test set rows:", nrow(test_df), "\n")
cat("bess_probs length:", length(bess_probs), "| y_test length:", length(y_test), "\n")

# Plot BIC across subset sizes
plot(bess_model, main = "BeSS — BIC by Subset Size")
```

---

## 7. Random Forest

```{r Random-Forest}
set.seed(42)
rf_model <- randomForest(
  home_win ~ .,
  data       = train_df,
  ntree      = 500,
  mtry       = floor(sqrt(ncol(train_df) - 1)),
  importance = TRUE
)

print(rf_model)

rf_probs <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_df, type = "prob")[, "Win"]
rf_eval  <- evaluate_model(rf_probs, y_test, "Random Forest")
```

```{r RF-Variable-Importance}
# Variable importance (Mean Decrease in Gini)
imp_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rf_model)) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
  arrange(desc(MeanDecreaseGini))

ggplot(imp_df, aes(x = reorder(feature, MeanDecreaseGini), y = MeanDecreaseGini)) +
  geom_col(fill = "forestgreen") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest — Variable Importance (Mean Decrease Gini)",
       x = "Feature", y = "Mean Decrease Gini") +
  theme_minimal()
```

---

## 8. Model Comparison

```{r ROC-Comparison}
# Overlay ROC curves for all models
roc_log   <- log_eval$roc
roc_lasso <- lasso_eval$roc
#roc_bess  <- bess_eval$roc
roc_rf    <- rf_eval$roc

plot(roc_log,   col = "blue",   lwd = 2,
     main = "ROC Curves — All Models")
plot(roc_lasso, col = "red",    lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
#plot(roc_bess,  col = "purple", lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
plot(roc_rf,    col = "green",  lwd = 2, add = TRUE)
abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2, col = "gray")
legend("bottomright",
       legend = c(
         paste0("Logistic  (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_log),   3), ")"),
         paste0("LASSO     (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_lasso), 3), ")"),
         #paste0("BeSS      (AUC = ", round(auc(roc_bess),  3), ")"), can't get BeSS to work for unknown reason
         paste0("Rand. For.(AUC = ", round(auc(roc_rf),    3), ")")
       ),
       col = c("blue", "red", "purple", "green"),
       lwd = 2)
```
